proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 80 binary patient features, feature_035 (prevalence ~7.2%) is associated with HIGHER objective_response than the cohort baseline of ~16.9%.
Most binary features have no detectable main-effect association with objective_response — the signal in this dataset is concentrated in a small number of biomarkers.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_035, objective_response))
analyses.result_summary: feature_035: response rate 0.2213 when feature_035=1 (n=3624) vs 0.1650 when feature_035=0; diff=+0.0563, chi2 p=3.52e-18. feature_035=1 is associated with a +5.6 percentage-point absolute increase in response.

analyses.code: for each of 80 binary features: chi2_contingency(crosstab(f, y)); count p<0.05.
analyses.result_summary: Number of binary features significant at p<0.05: 4/80; at p<0.001: 1/80; at Bonferroni 0.05/80=6.25e-4: 1/80. Only feature_035 survives Bonferroni correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, range 20-100, mean ~60 — plausibly a percent / hematologic value) is associated with objective_response: responders have LOWER feature_006 than non-responders.
Older patients (higher feature_078, age in years) have LOWER probability of objective_response.
feature_011 (continuous, mean ~3.9, max 23.6 — plausibly a count/integer-like lab) is HIGHER in non-responders than responders.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_006); welch t-test of feature_006 by objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006: mean responders=0.655, non-responders=0.826, diff=-0.170, logit beta=-0.38374/unit, p=1.84e-97.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_078)
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 (age): mean responders=68.03, non-responders=67.98, diff=+0.05 years, logit beta=+0.00040/yr, p=7.18e-01.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_011)
analyses.result_summary: feature_011: mean responders=3.383, non-responders=3.952, diff=-0.569, logit beta=-0.0401/unit, p=7.43e-35.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2, prev ~35%/50%/15% — plausibly performance status) is NEGATIVELY associated with objective_response: response rate decreases monotonically as feature_057 goes from 0 to 2.
feature_018 (ordinal 0-10, mean ~3, plausibly a comorbidity / line-of-therapy / cytogenetic-risk count) is NEGATIVELY associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_057); response rate by level.
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 logit beta=-0.3837, p=1.84e-97. Response rates: level 0=0.213, level 1=0.155, level 2=0.112. Difference (level 2 - level 0)=-0.1010.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_018); response rate by level.
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 logit beta=+0.0042, p=3.40e-01. Response rate at level 0=0.168; at max level=0.190; diff=+0.0223.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rate differs across racial groups (feature_005); specifically Black patients have a LOWER unadjusted response rate than White patients.
Hispanic patients have a LOWER unadjusted response rate than White patients.
Asian patients have a HIGHER OR EQUAL unadjusted response rate than White patients.

analyses.code: chi2(feature_005, objective_response); pairwise white vs black.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response by race: white=0.1728 (n=32498), black=0.1574 (n=5889), hispanic=0.1670 (n=7631), asian=0.1633 (n=2970), other=0.1472 (n=1012). Overall chi2 p=9.59e-03; Black vs White: diff=-0.0154, p=4.00e-03.

analyses.code: Pairwise chi2 white vs hispanic.
analyses.result_summary: Hispanic vs White: diff=-0.0059, p=2.26e-01.

analyses.code: Pairwise chi2 white vs asian.
analyses.result_summary: Asian vs White: diff=-0.0095, p=1.96e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Medicaid-insured patients have a LOWER unadjusted response rate than privately-insured patients.
Uninsured patients have a LOWER unadjusted response rate than privately-insured patients.
Medicare-insured patients have a LOWER unadjusted response rate than privately-insured patients (likely confounded by older age in Medicare cohort).

analyses.code: chi2(feature_087, objective_response); pairwise medicaid vs private.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response by insurance: medicare=0.1718 (n=20953), private=0.1666 (n=20090), medicaid=0.1687 (n=6911), uninsured=0.1662 (n=2046). Overall chi2 p=5.40e-01. Medicaid vs Private: diff=+0.0022, p=6.91e-01.

analyses.code: Pairwise chi2 uninsured vs private.
analyses.result_summary: Uninsured vs Private: diff=-0.0004, p=9.90e-01.

analyses.code: Pairwise chi2 medicare vs private.
analyses.result_summary: Medicare vs Private: diff=+0.0053, p=1.59e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age (feature_078), top biomarkers (feature_006, _011, _057, _018, _099, _063, _092, _035), and insurance, the Black-vs-White disparity in objective_response PERSISTS — i.e., feature_005=='black' has a significantly negative coefficient relative to white reference.
After the same adjustment, the Medicaid-vs-Private disparity in objective_response PERSISTS.
After biomarker adjustment, the Medicare-vs-Private disparity ATTENUATES toward zero, consistent with the gap being driven by older age in Medicare patients.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ age + biomarkers + race + insurance), white = race reference.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted (n=50000, pseudo-R2=0.0186): race_black beta=-0.1093, p=5.06e-03 (OR=0.896); race_hispanic beta=-0.0358, p=2.97e-01; race_asian beta=-0.0793, p=1.29e-01.

analyses.code: Same model; examine ins_medicaid coefficient (private = reference).
analyses.result_summary: ins_medicaid beta=+0.0205, p=5.86e-01 (OR=1.021); ins_uninsured beta=+0.0098, p=8.76e-01.

analyses.code: Compare unadjusted Medicare vs Private (iter 5) to adjusted ins_medicare coefficient.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted Medicare-Private absolute diff was +0.0053 (p=1.59e-01); adjusted ins_medicare beta=+0.0419, p=1.15e-01 (OR=1.043). Attenuated: no.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features with prevalence near 50% (candidates for biological sex), feature_002 (~47% prev) is associated with objective_response.
feature_122 (~45% prev) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_002, objective_response))
analyses.result_summary: feature_002: response rate 0.1674 when feature_002=1 vs 0.1705 when feature_002=0; diff=-0.0031, chi2 p=3.70e-01.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_122, objective_response))
analyses.result_summary: feature_122: response rate 0.1683 when feature_122=1 vs 0.1696 when feature_122=0; diff=-0.0013, chi2 p=7.02e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 (positive predictor) on objective_response varies by age (feature_078) — specifically, the absolute response-rate increase from feature_035=1 is LARGER in younger patients (lower-third age) than in older patients.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_035 * feature_078); compare stratified response-rate diff across age tertiles.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.00039, p=0.920. Stratified diffs (rate at f035=1 minus f035=0): young: +0.0502 (n=16757); mid: +0.0622 (n=16590); old: +0.0566 (n=16653)

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_035 on objective_response is HOMOGENEOUS across racial groups (feature_005); i.e., no significant feature_035 × race interaction.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_035 * race); Wald joint test of interaction terms.
analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald p for feature_035:race interactions = 0.728. Stratified diffs (f035=1 minus f035=0): white: +0.0646 (n=32498); black: +0.0409 (n=5889); hispanic: +0.0410 (n=7631); asian: +0.0396 (n=2970)

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association of feature_057 with objective_response is consistent across racial groups (no feature_057 × race interaction).

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_057 * race); Wald joint test of interaction; race-stratified slopes.
analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald p for feature_057:race = 0.825. Race-stratified feature_057 slopes: white: beta=-0.377 p=4.44e-63 n=32498; black: beta=-0.441 p=1.90e-15 n=5889; hispanic: beta=-0.382 p=4.99e-16 n=7631; asian: beta=-0.381 p=4.48e-07 n=2970

proposed_hypotheses.text: The right-skewed feature_092 (max 500, mean 20) has a stronger logistic association with objective_response on the log scale than on the linear scale (lower AIC).

analyses.code: Compare AIC: logit(y ~ feature_092) vs logit(y ~ log1p(feature_092)).
analyses.result_summary: Linear: beta=-0.00216, p=1.88e-05, AIC=45420.6. Log: beta=-0.0785, p=4.35e-09, AIC=45405.4. Better fit: log by ΔAIC=+15.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response rate decreases monotonically across quartiles of feature_006 (i.e., the relationship is approximately monotone, not U-shaped or threshold-only).

analyses.code: Quartiles of feature_006; mean response per quartile; linear trend test.
analyses.result_summary: Response rate by quartile (Q1 lowest f006 → Q4 highest): Q1=0.1896, Q2=0.1748, Q3=0.1650, Q4=0.1467. Linear-trend slope across quartiles=-0.0139, p=6.18e-03; logit beta=-0.00567.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After biomarker adjustment, the joint effect of race (4 dummies vs white) on objective_response remains statistically significant.
After biomarker adjustment, the joint effect of insurance (3 dummies vs private) on objective_response remains statistically significant.

analyses.code: Wald joint test on race dummies in adjusted model.
analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald chi2=13.27 (df=4), p=1.00e-02.

analyses.code: Wald joint test on insurance dummies in adjusted model.
analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald chi2=2.52 (df=3), p=4.72e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_057 on objective_response is similar magnitude in feature_035=0 and feature_035=1 subgroups (no significant interaction).

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_057 * feature_035); compare slopes by f035 stratum.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.0270, p=0.674. Stratified slopes for feature_057: f035=0 beta=-0.385 p=1.78e-89 n=46376; f035=1 beta=-0.358 p=4.73e-09 n=3624

proposed_hypotheses.text: The age effect on objective_response (negative slope of feature_078) is consistent across racial groups (no age × race interaction).

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_078 * race); Wald joint test on interaction; race-stratified age slopes.
analyses.result_summary: Joint Wald p for feature_078:race interactions = 0.343. Race-stratified age slopes: white: beta=+0.00203 p=1.32e-01 n=32498; black: beta=-0.00283 p=3.89e-01 n=5889; hispanic: beta=-0.00189 p=5.01e-01 n=7631; asian: beta=-0.00496 p=2.75e-01 n=2970

proposed_hypotheses.text: Modeling feature_018 as a continuous linear effect is adequate — categorical (factor) representation does not significantly improve fit (likelihood-ratio p > 0.05).

analyses.code: LR test: logit(y ~ feature_018) vs logit(y ~ C(feature_018)).
analyses.result_summary: LR statistic=5.89, df=9.0, p=0.751. AIC linear=45439.2, AIC factor=45451.3. Linear is adequate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035 retains a positive, statistically-significant association with objective_response after adjustment for age, top biomarkers, race, and insurance.

analyses.code: Compare unadjusted logit(y~feature_035) to adjusted multivariable model coefficient.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: beta=+0.3637, p=4.13e-18, OR=1.439. Adjusted (full multivariable model): beta=+0.3615, p=1.34e-17, OR=1.436.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combined disadvantage of race+insurance is largest for Black/Medicaid patients vs White/Private patients (largest absolute response-rate gap).

analyses.code: Compare response rate Black/Medicaid vs White/Private subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Black/Medicaid: rate=0.1585 (n=839); White/Private: rate=0.1703 (n=13111); diff=-0.0118, chi2 p=4.04e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Black patients differ from White patients in baseline feature_018 (mean) — i.e., racial disparities in disease severity at presentation may partly explain the disparity in response.

analyses.code: Welch t-test feature_018 black vs white; same for feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, mean ~3.8, range 1.7-5.5) is POSITIVELY associated with objective_response — higher feature_099 predicts higher response probability.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_099)
analyses.result_summary: feature_099: mean responders=3.825, non-responders=3.796; logit beta=+0.1151/unit, p=1.36e-06; OR per unit=1.122.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 and feature_011 (both negatively associated with response) interact: the joint negative effect is super-additive, i.e., a positive interaction coefficient on the logit scale (or equivalently, sub-additive risk on the rate scale).

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_006 * feature_011)
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.000285, p=0.093. Main effects in interaction model: f006 beta=-0.00672, f011 beta=-0.0571.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The multivariable model combining top biomarkers + demographics achieves AUC > 0.60 for predicting objective_response, materially better than the best single feature.

analyses.code: roc_auc_score on predictions from the multivariable adjusted model and individual top features.
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable AUC=0.5975. Single features: feature_057 AUC=0.4336; feature_006 AUC=0.5320; feature_011 AUC=0.5389; feature_018 AUC=0.4969.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adjusting for age (feature_078) substantially attenuates the Medicare-vs-Private gap (a confounding signal), but does NOT eliminate the Medicaid-vs-Private gap.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ insurance) vs logit(y ~ age + insurance); compare Medicare and Medicaid coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: Medicare: unadjusted beta=+0.0374, age-adjusted beta=+0.0375 (attenuation=-0.0000). Medicaid: unadjusted beta=+0.0155, age-adjusted beta=+0.0155.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The number of features showing a univariate association with objective_response that survives FDR (BH q<0.05) is small — fewer than 15 of the ~120 features.

analyses.code: multipletests(all_univariate_pvals, method='fdr_bh').
analyses.result_summary: Tested 123 features. Significant uncorrected p<0.05: 12; BH FDR q<0.05: 7; Bonferroni p<4.07e-04: 7.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The Black-vs-White disparity in objective_response is consistent in direction across feature_057 strata (i.e., the disparity is not concentrated in one performance-status / severity subgroup).

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_057; compute Black vs White rate diff in each stratum.
